texto: Freedom like charity, begins at home. No man is worthy to fight in the cause of freedom unless he has conquered his internal masters. He must learn control and discipline over the disastrous passions that would lead him to folly and ruin. He must conquer inordinate vanity and anger, self-deception, fear, and inhibition.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Jack Whiteside Parsons: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de enfrentar incertezas com ação. O trecho “Freedom like charity, begins at home. No man is worthy to fight in the cause of freedom unless he…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que autoestima, discipline, freedom pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de freedom e self-control.
